when asked "Check if patient S3241217 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order," first search for the catheter insertion procedure using the correct code (e.g., NUR1373) to get the most recent insertion date and time; if zero results are found, do not assume no catheter exists—instead, report that no insertion record was found and therefore cannot determine if a catheter is in place, and do not create a removal service request.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S0547588 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first search for the actual vaccination record for that specific patient using the Immunization resource with the correct COVID-19 vaccine codes; if no record is found, report that no vaccination record was found and explicitly state that no booster will be ordered, rather than stating "No COVID-19 vaccination record found" without the explicit statement that no booster will be ordered.

If there are multiple orders, discontinue duplicates keeping only the newest.", first search for all active DVT prophylaxis orders for that specific patient using the correct medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, or other DVT prophylaxis agents) and count them from the initial search results — do not search for all active medication orders and then scan through non-DVT medications or continue to the next page; instead, confirm the count of active DVT prophylaxis orders directly from the focused search results, then either create one order if zero are found, discontinue all duplicate orders keeping only the newest if multiple are found, or report that the requirement is satisfied if exactly one is found.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S1152319 and order a new one if performed more than 12 months ago, you must first search for the procedure using the correct CPT codes (IMGCT0491 or IMGIL0001) to retrieve the actual study date; if no procedure is found, report that no procedure was found and explicitly state that no new study will be ordered, rather than reporting "No CT Abdomen procedure found" and failing to include the explicit statement that no new study will be ordered.
